label: Hong Kong Entertainment Expo 2026 launches with focus on generative AI
label_zh: 香港影視博覽2026開幕，聚焦生成式AI

timeline.title: Hong Kong Entertainment Expo 2026 Debuts AI Academy to Transform Asian Film Production
timeline.title_zh: 香港國際影視展2026推出AI學院，冀轉型亞洲電影製作
timeline.sub_title: Marking its 30th anniversary, the Expo integrates generative AI through a dedicated AI Hub and new training workshops to empower global filmmakers and streamline creative workflows.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 展覽迎來30週年，透過專設的AI Hub及全新培訓工作坊整合生成式AI，賦予全球電影製作人力量，並簡化創意工作流程。
timeline.summary:
The Hong Kong Entertainment Expo 2026 has officially unveiled a landmark program for its upcoming season, positioning generative artificial intelligence as the central pillar of the city's creative future. Running from March 15 to April 19, this year’s Expo celebrates major milestones, including the 30th anniversary of the Hong Kong International Film and TV Market (FILMART) and the 50th edition of the Hong Kong International Film Festival (HKIFF). Organizers [ref:1] have confirmed that the 2026 event will host over 790 exhibitors from 30 countries and regions, reinforcing Hong Kong’s status as the leading entertainment hub in Asia.

A standout development for the 2026 edition is the massive expansion of the AI Hub and the inauguration of the inaugural AI Academy. Funded by the Cultural and Creative Industries Development Agency (CCIDA) and the Film Development Fund, the AI Academy is set to host over 15 specialized workshops. these [ref:2] sessions are designed to provide creative professionals with hands-on experience in using AI engines for scriptwriting, storyboard generation, and virtual production. Leading technology firms such as Alibaba Cloud, MiniMax, and Vidu will showcase their latest solutions, demonstrating how autonomous AI agents can automate laborious production tasks while preserving the artistic vision of human directors.

Technological innovation will take center stage during the EntertainmentPulse conference, particularly with the introduction of Kling AI’s latest models, Kling 3.0 and Kling 3.0 Omni. These tools aim to democratize high-end filmmaking under the theme "Everyone is a Director," offering advanced features like subject reference technology for visual consistency and intelligent storyboarding. A highlight [ref:3] of the programming includes a deep dive into the "Human-in-the-Loop" model, where global industry pioneers will discuss the delicate balance of integrating generative tools into high-end production pipelines without sacrificing cinematic craft.

In addition to its focus on technology, the Expo continues to foster international business through the return of the Producers Connect program. This initiative will bring together more than 100 global and local producers to explore co-production opportunities and regional funding. The event will also feature 42 shortlisted projects from the Hong Kong-Asia Film Financing Forum (HAF), many of which are animation and genre-driven entries that highlight the growing diversity of Asian storytelling. With 30 regional pavilions representing markets such as France, the United Kingdom, and the United States, the Expo is poised to facilitate significant cross-border intellectual property trading.

Ambassador Leon Lai returns to champion the city's creative output, which this year includes high-profile nominations for the Hong Kong Film Awards and the 19th Asian Film Awards. As the industry grapples with the shift toward digital transformation, the Entertainment Expo 2026 serves as a critical testing ground for the convergence of traditional filmmaking and the next generation of AI-driven media, ensuring that the region remains at the forefront of global entertainment innovation.
timeline.summary_zh:
2026香港國際影視展已為即將到來的一季正式揭開了具有里程碑意義的計劃，將生成式人工智能定位為該市創意未來的核心支柱。本屆展覽將於3月15日至4月19日舉行，慶祝香港國際影視展（FILMART）成立30週年和香港國際電影節（HKIFF）舉辦50屆等重要里程碑。主辦方已確認，2026年的活動將匯聚來自30個國家和地區的790多家參展商，鞏固香港作為亞洲領先娛樂中心的地位。[ref:1]

2026年展覽的突出發展是AI Hub的大幅擴展以及首屆AI學院的成立。AI學院由文化創意產業發展機構（CCIDA）和電影發展基金資助，將舉辦超過15個專題工作坊。這些課程旨在為創意專業人士提供使用AI引擎進行劇本撰寫、故事板生成和虛擬製作的實踐經驗。阿里巴巴雲、MiniMax和Vidu等領先技術公司將展示其最新解決方案，演示自動化AI代理如何在保留人類導演的藝術願景的同時，自動化費力的製作任務。[ref:2]

技術創新將在EntertainmentPulse會議上佔據中心舞台，特別是Kling AI的最新模型Kling 3.0和Kling 3.0 Omni的推出。這些工具旨在以「人人都是導演」為主題，通過主題參考技術實現視覺一致性以及智能故事板等先進功能，從而實現高端電影製作的民主化。節目的一大亮點是對「人機協同」模式的深入探討，全球行業先驅將討論將生成工具整合到高端製作流程中而不犧牲電影工藝之間的微妙平衡。[ref:3]

除了專注於技術之外，展覽還通過「製片人聯繫」（Producers Connect）計劃繼續促進國際商業合作。該計劃將匯集超過100位全球和本地製片人，共同探討聯合製作機會和區域資金。該活動還將展出來自香港亞洲電影投資會（HAF）的42個入圍項目，其中許多是動畫和類型片，突顯了亞洲敘事的日益多元化。來自法國、英國和美國等市場的30個地區展館，預計將促進重大的跨境知識產權貿易。

大使黎明將回歸推廣該市的創意產出，今年的作品包括香港電影獎和第19屆亞洲電影獎的高關注度提名。隨著行業應對向數字化轉型的趨勢，2026香港國際影視展將成為傳統電影製作與下一代AI驅動媒體融合的關鍵試驗場，確保該地區始終處於全球娛樂創新前沿。

timeline.title: Hong Kong Entertainment Expo 2026 Unveils AI-Driven Vision and 50th Anniversary Film Festival Lineup
timeline.title_zh: 香港2026娛樂博覽揭示AI驅動願景及50周年電影節陣容
timeline.sub_title: The territory’s premier entertainment event integrates advanced generative AI workshops and multi-regional partnerships, marking significant milestones for the 30th FILMART and the 50th Hong Kong International Film Festival.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 該地區首屈一指的娛樂盛事整合了先進的生成式AI工作坊和多區域合作夥伴關係，標誌著第30屆電影市場及第50屆香港國際電影節邁入重要里程碑。
timeline.summary:
The Hong Kong Entertainment Expo 2026 has officially detailed its comprehensive program, signaling a transformative shift toward generative artificial intelligence as it prepares to celebrate several major milestones. Spearheaded by the Hong Kong Trade Development Council, the 2026 edition spans from mid-March through late April, uniting eight flagship events including the landmark 30th edition of FILMART and the golden 50th anniversary of the Hong Kong International Film Festival. At [ref:4] a major media launch held in anticipation of the kick-off, organizers emphasized Hong Kong's evolving role as a bridge between traditional cinematic artistry and the rapidly advancing frontier of digital technology.

A central pillar of this year’s expo is the newly inaugurated AI Academy, a dedicated initiative funded by the Cultural and Creative Industries Development Agency and the Film Development Fund. The academy is set to host more than 15 thematic workshops designed to train industry professionals in the practical application of generative AI tools. These [ref:3] sessions will cover the entire production pipeline, from intelligent storyboarding and character consistency to voice synthesis and post-production visual effects. Complementing this educational push, the expanded AI Hub will showcase innovations from global technology leaders such as Alibaba Cloud, Kling AI, and MiniMax. Key highlights [ref:2, 1, 3, 4] include the unveiling of the Kling 3.0 Omni model, which promises to redefine the role of directors by streamlining the transition from conceptual prompts to high-quality screen content.

The 30th edition of FILMART remains the expo's commercial engine, attracting over 790 exhibitors from 30 countries and regions. This year’s market features an upgraded Producers Connect program, facilitating cross-regional co-productions by bringing together over 100 international and local producers. The event [ref:4] also highlights the growth of the Greater Bay Area’s creative ecosystem, with specialized sessions focusing on how AI-driven intellectual property strategies can accelerate the globalization of short dramas and animated content originating from Shenzhen, Hong Kong, and Guangzhou.

On the cultural front, the 50th Hong Kong International Film Festival has adopted the forward-looking theme of Framing the Future. The festival will open with the highly anticipated world premiere of the drama We’re All Strangers and conclude with the transgender-themed narrative Cyclone. During the recent media launch, directors and stars gathered to celebrate the festival’s rich heritage while embracing its new commitment to innovation. This commitment is further exemplified by the separate Hong Kong AI International Film Festival occurring later in the spring, which introduces a native evaluation system for AI-generated works, moving the industry toward what proponents call a conductor theory of filmmaking.

With Leon Lai returning as the Hong Kong Entertainment Ambassador, the expo aims to reinforce the city's status as a premier global hub for the creative industries. By blending the prestige of the Hong Kong Film Awards and the Asian Film Awards with cutting-edge summits on digital entertainment, the 2026 Expo seeks to provide a definitive blueprint for how the global entertainment sector can harness emerging technologies to expand its narrative and commercial reach.
timeline.summary_zh:
香港2026娛樂博覽已正式公佈其綜合節目，預示著在慶祝多個重大里程碑之際，將向生成式人工智能進行轉型。由香港貿易發展局牽頭，2026年展覽橫跨三月中旬至四月下旬，匯集了八個旗艦活動，包括標誌性的第30屆電影市場（FILMART）及寶貴的第50屆香港國際電影節。在展覽開幕前舉行的一場主要媒體發佈會上，主辦方強調香港作為傳統電影藝術與快速發展的數字技術前沿之間的橋樑，其日益演變的角色。[ref:4]

今年博覽會的核心支柱是新成立的AI學院，這是一項由創意產業發展基金和電影發展基金資助的專項計劃。該學院將舉辦超過15場主題工作坊，旨在培訓行業專業人士掌握生成式AI工具的實際應用。這些課程將涵蓋整個製作流程，從智能故事板和角色一致性，到語音合成和後期視覺特效。為配合這一教育推動，擴展的AI中心將展示來自阿里巴巴雲、Kling AI和MiniMax等全球技術領導者的創新成果。主要亮點包括Kling 3.0 Omni模型的發布，該模型承諾通過簡化從概念提示到高質量螢幕內容的轉變，重新定義導演的角色。[ref:3, 2, 1, 4]

第30屆電影市場（FILMART）仍是展覽的商業引擎，吸引了來自30個國家和地區的790多家參展商。今年的電影市場推出升級版的製片人連線計劃，匯集了100多位國際和本地製片人，促進跨區域聯合製作。該活動還強調了大灣區創意生態系統的成長，專門舉辦了聚焦AI驅動的知識產權戰略如何加速源自深圳、香港和廣州的短劇和動畫內容的全球化。[ref:4]
